node_2: scalable, detailed, and mask-free universal photometric stereo network
edge: In this paper, we present SDM-UniPS, a photometric stereo network that recovers normal maps with remarkable accuracy from images captured under extremely uncontrolled lighting conditions.
SDM-UniPS enables the generation of normal maps with higher resolution than the training data.
SDM-UniPS provides more accurate normal maps on DiLiGenT with a limited number of input images than most existing orthographic photometric stereo techniques.
In some cases, SDM-UniPS surpasses 3D scanners in detail.
SDM-UniPS is mask-free, allowing for application even when masks are absent, unlike many conventional methods.
SDM-UniPS introduces a scale-invariant spatial-light feature encoder that efficiently extracts illumination features while maintaining scalability with respect to input image size.
SDM-UniPS introduces a novel surface normal decoder utilizing a pixel-sampling transformer to predict surface normals through non-local interactions.
SDM-UniPS created a new synthetic training dataset comprising multiple objects with diverse textures rendered under varied lighting conditions.
Photometric stereo techniques significantly reduce the time required for data acquisition compared to existing methods.
Photometric stereo techniques contribute to faster normal map recovery without requiring meticulous lighting control.
